candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary row above EBIT, but it is not labelled as cost of sales / COGS-related. Using it for COGS would risk misallocating company-wide operating D&A to the COGS bucket.
Component of the D&A summary row, but not specifically tied to cost of sales and therefore not appropriate for separate COGS addition.
Component of the D&A summary row, but not specifically tied to cost of sales and therefore not appropriate for separate COGS addition.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is 'Cost of sales'. The excerpt shows only a generic operating D&A line and its components, with no wording indicating 'in COGS' or similar. Conservatively treat COGS D&A as already embedded in 'Cost of sales' or not separately identifiable, so no separate COGS_DA row should be added.

candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A summary row appears separately alongside 'Other external expenses' and 'Administrative Exp.' above EBIT. Since the selected XSGA bucket excludes depreciation/amortization and no parent SG&A total was selected, this separate summary row should be added later to XSGA rather than its components.
Component of the separate D&A summary row. Do not select if the summary row is used, to avoid double counting.
Component of the separate D&A summary row. Do not select if the summary row is used, to avoid double counting.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA reference bucket is the combination of 'Administrative Exp.' and 'Other external expenses'. 'Depreciation and amortization' is presented as a separate same-level operating line above EBIT, not as a child of either selected row. Under the summary-vs-components rule, use only the summary row and exclude its component rows.
